notes: Kenya should give full credit to both English and Swahili because they are the country's two official languages and both are highly relevant for traveler communication across the country.

evidence.title: Magical Kenya: Plan your trip
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance says the two official languages in Kenya are English and Swahili.

evidence.title: Magical Kenya Mombasa brochure
evidence.note: Official tourism materials explicitly teach visitors basic Swahili phrases, supporting Swahili as a live traveler-relevant language.

notes: Zambia should score English as the official traveler-facing language while recognizing that large local languages such as Bemba and Nyanja matter strongly in everyday life. Those local-language exact-code assignments can be expanded later in the reconciliation pass.

evidence.title: Zambia Tourism: Facts about Zambia
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance lists English as Zambia's official language and Bemba as the most spoken local language.

evidence.title: Zambia Tourism: Tourist Guide general info
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance says English is the official language, that business and media are conducted in English, and that most hospitality workers speak it fairly well.
